transcript.clean_text: vegeta italia rolled onto day 12 after stage 11 had ended in a bunch sprint a bunch sprint won by a brand new rider at this level alberto dainese winning for the first time in a grand tour the young italian picking up his nation's first victory at their home race a day later we were due to head to the seaside leaving parma heading over the hills not so far away but finishing on the longest stage of the stura d'italia after 204 kilometers in the city of genoa dsm void from their victory signed in matcha fonda pool will be hoping to try and do something and the first big climb coming after 90 kilometers but it was all uphill from the start descendingly passo del bocco bringing poignant and tragic memories after the passing of walter velonte on that descent the last time it was used in 2011. two more climbs after that a finish in genoa over the brand new ponte san pietro in the middle of the city what a fight it was for the breakaway average speed in the first hour 55 kilometers an hour arnold de mar took the bunch sprint for the intermediate sprint 12 points and the brakes still haven't gone after 56 case vista bond tried to force it after that and it would be 70 kilometers before a group of 25 riders formed before then richard carrapaz had even tried a cheeky attack and once the group got organized they fairly quickly picked up a decent advantage badayani had missed it however and two of their artists caburo and kovili would work hard with zardini of drone opera and ronnie jocatli to get across to the leaders they took on the descent of paso del boco together and maro venue the race leader commemorated the tragic loss of water welland laying a wreath 11 years on but not forgotten into the final 60 kilometers and the stage win was played out already penultimate classified climb and lorenzo rota going off the front he'd be followed soon by stefan aldani and his lame racer and with 50 kilometers to go they managed to build up an advantage of just over 20 seconds chase's behind would come and go but it would be reasonable who would force the issue withdrago who would come with him the leaders though by this point having almost a minute of a gap withrago joined by hamilton alongside kelderman 11 minutes was the best placed in the gc and balcomolomer looking for his first ever gelos stage win to add to those in the tour in the welter inside the final 20 kilometers the gap at under 50 seconds but it was still over 40 with six k's to go the three leaders went into the final kilometer all hoping for the first ever professional bike wins liam does have tried twice in the last k not trusting his own speed it proved to be the right decision even though he didn't win it was stefan aldani who was quite clearly the quickest he got the better of lorenzo rota and oldani the italian picking up not only his first professional victory as a cyclist but also a first jiro d'italia win the second for alpacine fenix this giro aldani rota and lembres of the podium on the day with kelvin behind taking eight minutes back in the gc alongside lucas hamilton aldani taking the stage win rota in second place lemurism olymputrago and the rest inghorn completing the top 10 at almost three minutes the peloton rolled over over nine minutes down and juan pelopez secured a ninth day in the lead of the giro d'italia another maya rosa for the 24 year old spaniard so karapas and almeida still there at 12 seconds with balde and hindley in the top five separated only by 20 seconds at the stage 12 stage 13 takes on the summer pandemic sambremo route in reverse we go from san remo over the hills back on to flatter terrain we head into piamonte a stage finishing corneal that could and should be one for the sprinters but at this stage of the race the breakaways are hard fought it's a shorter day 150 k's long finishing in kunil you can catch each and every stage of the jira d'italia live or on demand on gcn plus discovery plus or on eurosport you
